attributes.stem: A 45-year-old woman is admitted to hospital with community-acquired pneumonia. A physician trainee conducting a research project has been having difficulty recruiting sufficient participants. To increase data collection, the trainee instructs the intern to order additional blood tests for research purposes on all admitted patients, including this woman, without obtaining consent. The intern has raised concerns with the trainee but these have not been addressed.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following is the most appropriate next step for the intern?
options: Obtain verbal consent from the patients
Obtain written consent from the patients
Contact the research ethics committee secretary
attributes.explanation:
This scenario describes a clear breach of research ethics. All research involving human subjects must receive prior approval from an ethics committee and requires informed consent from participants unless specifically waived.

Contacting the research ethics committee secretary is the most appropriate action as they have oversight of research conduct and can investigate potential breaches. The ethics committee can provide guidance, protect patient rights, and ensure proper procedures are followed.

Seeking advice from the consultant may not resolve the issue, particularly if they are involved in the research project. The ethics committee provides independent oversight specifically for research-related concerns.

Complying with the request would make the intern complicit in unethical research conduct. Healthcare workers have a professional obligation to report research misconduct.

Obtaining verbal consent or written consent from patients would not be appropriate as the intern may not be approved to obtain research consent. Additionally, the underlying issue of conducting unapproved research needs to be addressed through proper channels.

Research ethics committees exist to protect patient rights and ensure research is conducted ethically. They review protocols, consent processes, and investigator qualifications before research begins. Any significant protocol changes must receive additional approval.
learningPoints: All research involving human subjects requires prior ethics committee approval.
Informed consent is mandatory for research participation unless specifically waived by an ethics committee.
Healthcare workers have an ethical obligation to report research misconduct.
references: Scott AM, Kolstoe, S, Ploem MC, et al. Exempting low-risk health and medical research from ethics reviews: comparing Australia, the United Kingdom, the United States and the Netherlands. Health Research Policy and Systems. 2020; 18:11-19.
